articles.headline: Could Thomas Partey's World Cup absence be a blessing in disguise for Ghana?
articles.description: When Carlos Queiroz named Thomas Partey in Ghana's FIFA World Cup squad, the logic was clear... as were the risks. Will his absence against Panama prove to be a blessing, in retrospect?

images.name: Thomas Partey [1296x729] - Copy (2)
images.caption: Thomas Partey's absence might be an opportunity rather than a setback for Ghana.
images.alt: Thomas Partey of Ghana  during the international friendly match between Wales and Ghana at Cardiff City Stadium on June 02, 2026 in Cardiff, Wales

images.name: Jordan Ayew [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Jordan Ayew's performance will be particularly key in the absence of Thomas Partey.

images.name: Mark Ogden takes driverless taxi to U.S. vs. Paraguay

articles.headline: France vs. Senegal - kick-off time, team news, how to watch the FIFA World Cup
articles.description: France will face Senegal on Tuesday, June 16, in a FIFA World Cup Group I fixture of tremendous historic significance at MetLife Stadium.

images.name: Sadio Mané  [1296x729]
images.caption: Will Sadio Mané and Senegal be able to upset title favourites France?

images.name: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions
images.caption: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions

articles.headline: Morocco's FIFA World Cup run could define Achraf Hakimi's legacy
articles.description: Fresh from another Champions League triumph with Paris Saint-Germain -- with the rightback returning from injury to excel in the final -- Hakimi is the captain of the most accomplished Morocco side ever assembled.

images.name: Achraf Hakimi [1296x729]
images.caption: Achraf Hakimi: Footballing royalty.
images.credit: Leonardo MUNOZ / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Achraf Hakimi UCL trophy [1296x729]
images.caption: At just 27, can Achraf Hakimi overtake Samuel Eto'o as Africa's greatest player in terms of plaudits, especially if he adds another UEFA Champions League title to his name against Arsenal?

images.name: Enrique: PSG already working on the future after Champions League win
images.caption: Luis Enrique speaks about his plans for the PSG squad after their penalty shootout win over Arsenal in the Champions League final.

articles.headline: 'The Moroccan Bellingham', 'The Egyptian Haaland' - Picking one wonderkid from Africa's World Cup teams
articles.description: Here's one pick for each of Africa's 10 contenders to potentially be the revelation of the World Cup.

images.name: Relebohile Mofokeng [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Relebohile Mofokeng won the South African Premiership title with Orlando Pirates in 2026.
images.credit: Hector Vivas - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Hamza Abdelkarim of Egypt [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Opportunity knocks for Hamza Abdelkarim to impress alongside Mohamed Salah.
images.credit: Photo by Ayman Aref/NurPhoto via Getty Images

images.name: Ibrahim Mbaye [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Is Ibrahim Mbaye, indeed, Sadio Mane's natural successor?

images.name: Why Relebohile Mofokeng is South Africa's player to watch the World Cup
images.caption: Leonard Solms speaks about Relebohile Mofokeng after South Africa's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup was announced.

articles.headline: Introducing every single Premier League player representing an African team at the FIFA World Cup
articles.description: As ever, the Premier League is well represented across African national teams at the World Cup, with all but one of the content's 10 contenders featuring at least one player from the division.

images.name: Antoine Semenyo [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Ghana's FIFA World Cup campaign will be boosted by the presence of Manchester City's Antoine Semenyo.
images.credit: Photo by Alex Livesey - Danehouse/Getty Images

images.name: Amad celeb Ivory Coast_260106 [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Amad Diallo will welcome the opportunity to impress for Ivory Coast amid mixed results at Manchester United.
images.alt: Amad Diallo celebrates scoring a goal for Ivory Coast against Burkina Faso in round of 16 at AFCON.

images.name: Noah Sadiki [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Noah Sadiki was key to Sunderland's seventh-place finish in the English Premier League.

images.name: Mohamed Salah [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Whether Mohamed Salah will stay in the English Premier League remains in the balance.

images.name: Infantino: 'Unfortunate' to see Somali referee denied U.S. entry
images.caption: FIFA President Gianni Infantino says FIFA "does not control everything", after Somalian referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan was refused entry into the United States.

articles.headline: Ghana vs. Panama - kick-off time, team news, how to watch Black Stars' FIFA World Cup opener
articles.description: Ghana will kick off their FIFA World Cup campaign on Wednesday in Toronto, with a Group L clash against Panama.

images.name: Antoine Semenyo [1296x729]
images.caption: Antoine Semenyo is the key to Ghana's success at this FIFA World Cup, with the Manchester City striker arguably the best player in the English Premier League this year.

video.shortHeadline: Should Brazil be worried after Morocco draw?

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC_ANALYSIS (Should Brazil be worried after Morocco draw?) 2026_6_14

article.story:
Vinícius Júnior scored an equalizing goal in the 32nd minute, giving five-time champion Brazil a 1-1 draw against Morocco on Saturday in a pulsating, high-profile group match at the World Cup.

A semifinalist four years ago, Morocco dominated early, and Ismael Saibari put the African champions ahead in the 21st minute.

Brazil evened the score 11 minutes later. Vinícius exchanged passes with Bruno Guimarães on the left flank, took touches to make space and rifled a right-footed shot past the outstretched arm of Yassine Bounou for his 10th international goal.

Brazil, seeking its first World Cup title since 2002, entered the tournament with high expectations and yellow-clad Seleção fans who dominated the crowd of 80,663 at MetLife Stadium became a bit subdued before Vinícius evened the score with his spectacular angled effort.

No. 6 Brazil and seventh-ranked Morocco are the only top 10 teams to meet in the first round of the expanded 48-nation World Cup tournament.

Despite shaky moments, Brazil managed to extend its unbeaten streak in World Cup openers to 21, including 17 wins, since a 1934 loss to Spain.

Brazil plays Haiti on Friday in Philadelphia, then closes Group C against Scotland in Miami Gardens, Florida. Morocco faces Scotland in Foxborough, Massachusetts, on Friday, then meets Haiti in Atlanta.

Brazil star Neymar, recovering from a torn right calf, didn't dress for the game.

One hour before the match, an Uber from Times Square to the stadium, site of next month's final, was $126.95 -- up from $83.95 for advance booking for the same time Sunday.

Carlo Ancelotti, the Italian who became Brazil's first foreign World Cup coach, wore a three-piece suit with a necktie on a sunny afternoon with a 88-degree Fahrenheit (31-degree Celsius) temperature for the 6 p.m. start.

Morocco went ahead after Lucas Paquetá lost control of a short pass from Roger Ibañez and knocked it off Bilal El Khannouss. It bounced to Noussair Mazraoui, who sent the ball to Brahim Díaz in the center circle.

His through pass split Gabriel Magalhães and Marquinho, and Saibari ran onto the ball at the top of the arc. Alisson Becker was slow to come off his line and Saibari chipped the ball over the Brazil goalkeeper for his 10th international goal.
